title: The Temptation of Jesus part 6
scripture_text: ‘’Then Jesus was led by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil. After fasting forty days and forty nights, he was hungry. The tempter came to him and said, “If you are the Son of God, tell these stones to become bread.” Jesus answered, “It is written: ‘Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.’” Then the devil took him to the holy city and had him stand on the highest point of the temple. “If you are the Son of God, he said, “throw yourself down. For it is written: “‘He will command his angels concerning you, and they will lift you up in their hands, so that you will not strike your foot against a stone.’ Jesus answered him. ‘’It is also written: ‘Do not put the Lord your God to the test.’ Again, the devil took him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their splendour “All this I will give you,” he said, “if you will bow down and worship me.” Jesus said to him, “Away from me, Satan! For it is written: ‘Worship the Lord your God, and serve him only.’ Then the devil left him, and angels came and attended him.
devotional:
So how did Jesus respond to this temptation? Jesus said “Man does not live by bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.” Jesus’ answer is incredible; it exposes the faulty thinking of the tempter.

Jesus is saying, man needs more than bread to live. We have been made by God for God, so having our physical needs and desires met will never make us content. In saying that man does not live by bread alone, Jesus is saying that he really understands our humanity, he knows what makes humankind tick, he knows that mankind is more than a consumer. It is a satanic mindset that thinks that if I have all my physical hungers and desires met then I will be complete.

Sometimes as children of God we are asked to carry a hunger, an unfulfilled desire, an unmet need. As children of God there is no guarantee that all our prayers will be answered the way we want them to be. We are not promised a panacea, or a life free from suffering and pain. Why? Because we need to learn to love God as His children, we need to learn to love him unconditionally, to learn to love Him no matter what, learn to love Him for Himself, for that is the truest quality of a child of God, and the thing Satan fears most.

Trusting God is more important than food—more important, in fact, than anything else in life. That is the Saviour’s response. The devil is chiefly concerned with filling His empty stomach, but Jesus is chiefly concerned with trusting His Father who put Him in this spot in the first place. Dependence upon God in times of crisis is only possible if we are dependent when we are not in a crisis. One of the ways the children of God express their love for God is through depending on him.

When do we not need God? Many Christians live like functional atheists, they have a rational belief in God, but in practice they are independent and live as if they were self-sufficient.

As children of God we are designed to live in fellowship with God. ‘Man does not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.’ Often people use Jesus’ use of the scriptures to encourage people to memorise scripture; memorising scripture is a good thing, and should be encouraged, however, quoting the bible in the face of severe temptation is not a magic formula that will cause the tempter to go away. What Jesus is doing here is expressing his understanding of essential principles that shaped his life and thinking. Many people can quote the bible. But quoting the bible, and being shaped by the bible are very different.
prayer: Father thank you that my identity is important to you.
html:
The Temptation of Jesus part 6

Study Text: Matthew 4: 1-11
‘’Then Jesus was led by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil. After fasting forty days and forty nights, he was hungry. The tempter came to him and said, “If you are the Son of God, tell these stones to become bread.” Jesus answered, “It is written: ‘Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.’” Then the devil took him to the holy city and had him stand on the highest point of the temple. “If you are the Son of God, he said, “throw yourself down. For it is written: “‘He will command his angels concerning you, and they will lift you up in their hands, so that you will not strike your foot against a stone.’ Jesus answered him. ‘’It is also written: ‘Do not put the Lord your God to the test.’ Again, the devil took him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their splendour “All this I will give you,” he said, “if you will bow down and worship me.” Jesus said to him, “Away from me, Satan! For it is written: ‘Worship the Lord your God, and serve him only.’ Then the devil left him, and angels came and attended him.

So how did Jesus respond to this temptation? Jesus said “Man does not live by bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.” Jesus’ answer is incredible; it exposes the faulty thinking of the tempter.

Jesus is saying, man needs more than bread to live. We have been made by God for God, so having our physical needs and desires met will never make us content. In saying that man does not live by bread alone, Jesus is saying that he really understands our humanity, he knows what makes humankind tick, he knows that mankind is more than a consumer. It is a satanic mindset that thinks that if I have all my physical hungers and desires met then I will be complete.

Sometimes as children of God we are asked to carry a hunger, an unfulfilled desire, an unmet need. As children of God there is no guarantee that all our prayers will be answered the way we want them to be. We are not promised a panacea, or a life free from suffering and pain. Why? Because we need to learn to love God as His children, we need to learn to love him unconditionally, to learn to love Him no matter what, learn to love Him for Himself, for that is the truest quality of a child of God, and the thing Satan fears most.

Trusting God is more important than food—more important, in fact, than anything else in life. That is the Saviour’s response. The devil is chiefly concerned with filling His empty stomach, but Jesus is chiefly concerned with trusting His Father who put Him in this spot in the first place. Dependence upon God in times of crisis is only possible if we are dependent when we are not in a crisis. One of the ways the children of God express their love for God is through depending on him.

When do we not need God? Many Christians live like functional atheists, they have a rational belief in God, but in practice they are independent and live as if they were self-sufficient.

As children of God we are designed to live in fellowship with God. ‘Man does not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.’ Often people use Jesus’ use of the scriptures to encourage people to memorise scripture; memorising scripture is a good thing, and should be encouraged, however, quoting the bible in the face of severe temptation is not a magic formula that will cause the tempter to go away. What Jesus is doing here is expressing his understanding of essential principles that shaped his life and thinking. Many people can quote the bible. But quoting the bible, and being shaped by the bible are very different.

Prayer: Father thank you that my identity is important to you.
Bible in a year reading plan: 2 Samuel 23-24
